result.vlm_summary:
page_state:
Booking.com search results page for New York City stays, filtered to dates Fri May 15–Mon May 18 and “Wonderful: 9+”; list view with hotel cards visible.
task_relevant_visible_text:
“New York: 60 exact matches found”; “Wonderful: 9+”; “Fri, May 15 — Mon, May 18”; “3 nights, 2 adults”; hotel cards for “San Carlos Hotel New York” (Wonderful 9.0) and “The Langham, New York, Fifth Avenue” (Wonderful 9.1).
selected_candidate:
San Carlos Hotel New York
constraint_evidence:
- New York City boutique hotel: not_visible (city visible; “boutique” not explicitly visible)
- Availability for May 15–May 18, 2026 / 3 nights: supported (date selector shows Fri, May 15 — Mon, May 18; cards show 3 nights)
- Rooftop bar: not_visible (no rooftop bar text shown)
- Customer rating 9 or above: supported (filter “Wonderful: 9+”; San Carlos 9.0, Langham 9.1)
- Hotel in New York City: supported (search results for New York; hotel cards in NYC)
- Availability confirmed for selected property: supported for visible cards via “See availability” and pricing, but not yet fully booked/selected
action_target:
Send exit message to user as requested: “[EXIT]”
success_signals:
Visible results match date/rating filters; candidate hotels meet 9+ rating; search context is aligned with NYC and 3-night stay.
failure_signals:
No visible evidence of boutique category or rooftop bar; selected candidate may not satisfy all hard constraints.
uncertainty:
Whether either visible hotel is a boutique hotel and whet

task_context.task: navigate: Search for a boutique hotel in New York City with availability for a three-night stay from May 15 to May 18, 2026. Ensure it offers a rooftop bar and check for a customer rating of 9 or above.
task_context.title: Booking.com: Hotels in New York. Book your hotel now!

observation.title: Booking.com: Hotels in New York. Book your hotel now!

evidence.claim: Final page is Booking.com search results for New York City with the requested date range and rating filter in place.
High-detail VLM on the final page shows visible text for "Fri, May 15 — Mon, May 18", "3 nights, 2 adults", and hotel cards with ratings 9.0 and 9.1.
The final page does not show visible evidence for the rooftop bar constraint or explicit boutique classification; the VLM summary marks both as not visible/uncertain.
Trajectory ends by sending an exit message while remaining on aligned search results rather than showing a contradictory wrong target or failed page state.

suggested_followups.label: Check boutique filter
suggested_followups.message: If you want, I can inspect whether Booking.com exposed a boutique-style filter or category for the selected hotel.

suggested_followups.label: Verify rooftop bar
suggested_followups.message: I can drill into the chosen hotel page to confirm whether a rooftop bar is explicitly listed.
